title: White Gravy-cashew, cream, onion
description: White Gravy is a rich, creamy curry base made with cashews, onions, cream, and milk. It is perfect for paneer, chicken, kofta, and other mildly spiced Indian dishes.

ingredients.title: For the Base
items: 1/2 cup cashews (soaked in warm water for 30 min)

ingredients.title: For the Gravy
items: 1/4 tsp nutmeg (optional, very small pinch for aroma)

ingredients.title: For Tempering (Optional)
items: 1/2 tsp cumin seeds or whole green cardamom
A few curry leaves or crushed kasuri methi

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare Cashew-Onion Paste
steps: Blend cashews, onions, garlic, ginger, and 2–3 tbsp milk into a smooth, lump-free paste.
Add a little water if needed.

instruction.title: Step 2: Cook the Base
steps: Heat ghee or butter in a heavy-bottomed pan.
Add the cashew-onion paste and sauté on medium-low heat for 5–7 minutes, stirring continuously.
Do not let it brown—we want a white base.

instruction.title: Step 3: Make the Gravy
steps: Gradually add milk while stirring, ensuring a lump-free, smooth consistency.
Cook on low heat for 5–7 minutes until the mixture thickens slightly.

instruction.title: Step 4: Add Cream & Seasonings
steps: Stir in fresh cream and mix well.
Add salt, white pepper, cardamom powder, and nutmeg (if using).
Simmer for another 5 minutes on very low heat.

instruction.title: Step 5: Finish & Enhance Flavor
steps: Add 1–2 tsp lemon juice for freshness.
Optional tempering: Heat ghee, add cumin or cardamom, and pour over the gravy for aroma.

Serving Suggestions: Serve hot with vegetable kofta, paneer, or malai chicken.
Garnish with a swirl of cream and finely chopped coriander (optional).

chefTips: Use heavy cream for richness and silky texture.
Do not brown the paste—keep the heat low to maintain a white color.
Blanch cashews in hot water for 10 minutes before blending to make a smooth paste.
Strain the gravy if you want an ultra-smooth restaurant finish.
Slow cooking enhances flavor; avoid high heat.
